pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 11 and export_result.sh line 11 consistently use this path
docker-compose.yml (line 62 of setup) defines container_name: shadow-app, verified in export_result.sh line 10
server.js (line 19 of setup) sets const port = 3000, docker-compose.yml (line 64) maps ports 3000:3000, export_result.sh (line 25) tests http://localhost:3000
server.js (line 22 of setup) sends 'Hello from Docker!', verifier.py (line 56) validates this exact string
package.json (line 41 of setup) specifies express version ^4.18.2, confirmed as real npm package at https://www.npmjs.com/package/express/v/4.18.2

privileged_info_summary: This is a synthetic Docker/Node.js task testing volume shadowing bug fixes. The application project directory is /home/ga/projects/shadow-bug, created during setup with a Node.js/Express application. The Docker container is named shadow-app and listens on port 3000. The application responds with 'Hello from Docker!' when running correctly. The setup uses Express ^4.18.2 from npm, a real, publicly available package. The docker-compose.yml initially has a broken configuration where .:/app bind mount shadows the container's /app/node_modules directory, causing a module not found error on startup. All metadata values are consistent across setup, verification, and result export scripts.
